question: What techniques exist for self-improving agents without fine-tuning?

heuristic_evaluation.question: What techniques exist for self-improving agents without fine-tuning?

coverage.aspect: limits of self-improvement without external feedback

claim_lines: - **Verbal feedback / reflection loops:** the agent turns task feedback into natural-language reflections and stores them for later attempts. Reflexion is the canonical example. https://arxiv.org/abs/2303.11366

- **Self-refinement loops:** the same model critiques its own draft and revises iteratively; Self-Refine does this without supervised training, extra training, or RL. https://proceedings.neurips.cc/paper_files/paper/2023/hash/91edff07232fb1b55a505a9e9f6c0ff3-Abstract-Conference.html

- **Prompt/workflow optimization:** instead of changing weights, the system searches over prompts or instruction variants and keeps higher-scoring candidates. OPRO is the canonical prompt-optimization approach, and later ACL work shows small models may only get modest gains. https://arxiv.org/abs/2309.03409 https://aclanthology.org/2024.findings-acl.100/

- **Limits without external feedback:** purely intrinsic self-correction is fragile; one paper finds it can fail or degrade, while another says it can work only under careful settings like zero temperature and fair prompts. https://arxiv.org/abs/2310.01798 https://arxiv.org/abs/2406.15673
- verbal feedback — Reflexion explicitly uses verbal feedback signals and episodic memory to improve later actions. https://arxiv.org/abs/2303.11366
- reflection — Reflexion’s core mechanism is self-reflection on failures before the next attempt. https://arxiv.org/abs/2303.11366

- self-refinement — Self-Refine iteratively critiques and revises its own output without extra training. https://proceedings.neurips.cc/paper_files/paper/2023/hash/91edff07232fb1b55a505a9e9f6c0ff3-Abstract-Conference.html
- prompt or workflow optimization — OPRO iteratively optimizes instructions/prompts using prior candidates and scores. https://arxiv.org/abs/2309.03409

- limits of self-improvement without external feedback — self-correction is not reliably strong on its own and may require careful prompts/decoding. https://arxiv.org/abs/2310.01798 https://arxiv.org/abs/2406.15673

coverage_audit.reason: Directly describes verbal feedback loops and ties them to Reflexion.
Explicitly explains self-reflection on failures before the next attempt.
Clearly covers iterative critique-and-revise behavior without fine-tuning.
Covers prompt/instruction search and workflow optimization via OPRO and related work.

coverage_audit.aspect: limits of self-improvement without external feedback
coverage_audit.reason: Addresses fragility and setup sensitivity, though the discussion is somewhat broad and not deeply engineering-specific.

citation_audit.claim: Self-improving agents without fine-tuning usually fall into a few no-weight-update patterns.
citation_audit.reason: The cited papers collectively support the listed pattern categories, though the exact framing as a taxonomy is the answer's synthesis.

citation_audit.claim: Reflexion is the canonical example of verbal feedback / reflection loops.
citation_audit.reason: Reflexion clearly supports verbal feedback and reflection, but 'canonical example' is a strong characterization not directly evidenced by the citation.

citation_audit.claim: Self-Refine does iterative self-critique and revision without supervised training, extra training, or RL.
citation_audit.reason: The paper title/venue metadata plausibly support the method; the no-training/no-RL claim is consistent with the paper's stated contribution.

citation_audit.claim: OPRO searches over prompts or instruction variants and keeps higher-scoring candidates.
citation_audit.reason: This is the core idea of OPRO and is well matched to the cited source.

citation_audit.claim: Later ACL work shows small models may only have modest gains from prompt optimization.
citation_audit.reason: A Findings ACL paper on revisiting OPRO is a plausible source for limitations and reduced gains on smaller models.

citation_audit.claim: Purely intrinsic self-correction is fragile; one paper finds it can fail or degrade, while another says it can work only under careful settings like zero temperature and fair prompts.
citation_audit.reason: These papers are directly about self-correction ability/limits and plausibly support the stated caveat.

source_audit.reason: Primary paper for Reflexion; highly relevant to verbal feedback and reflection.
Primary venue page for Self-Refine; directly relevant.
Primary OPRO paper; directly relevant to prompt optimization.
Relevant follow-up paper on OPRO limitations; good source for trade-offs.
Primary source on inability to self-correct reasoning; relevant to limits.
Primary source on intrinsic self-correction ability; relevant to nuanced limits.

judge_evaluation.summary: A strong, well-structured answer that covers all required techniques and cites appropriate primary papers, with only mild overstatement in a few synthesis claims.
judge_evaluation.recommended_fix: Replace subjective or potentially overstated phrases like 'canonical example' with more precise wording and add one sentence explaining concrete engineering trade-offs and failure modes for each technique.
